subject: Databases
title: Graph Data in SQL
description: A quick win: understand Graph Data in SQL.

hook.text: Social networks: Alice follows Bob, Bob follows Carol. This isn't a hierarchy — it's a graph with arbitrary connections.

buildup.text: Model graphs with two tables: nodes (people) and edges (follows). Edges have from_id and to_id.

discovery.text: Finding friends-of-friends: join edges twice. Friends-of-friends-of-friends: three joins. N degrees: N joins.

twist.text: SQL handles graphs but poorly at scale. Graph databases (Neo4j) are built for deep traversals.

climax.text: For shallow graphs (2-3 hops), SQL is fine. For deep traversals, evaluate a graph database.

punchline.text: SQL models graphs with edge tables. For deep traversals, use graph databases.

quiz.question: Why does SQL struggle with deep graph traversals?
options: Each additional degree of connection requires another JOIN, making queries increasingly complex and slow
SQL cannot store graph data at all
Graph queries are always faster in SQL than in graph databases
